NKE.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with near-term caveats. S&P 500 at all-time highs above 7,200 on May 1, April 2026 was the best month since 2020. Fed holding at 3.5-3.75%, Iran peace talks reducing oil/energy tail risk. Macro wind is broadly at the back of risk assets, but Consumer Discretionary names with deteriorating fundamentals do not benefit proportionally in a risk-on environment driven by AI/tech. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: NKE is severely underperforming. ROC-21d is -16.02% while the S&P 500 just posted its best month since 2020. This is one of the widest divergences possible — NKE is in confirmed structural downtrend with EMA fully aligned bearish, price 24.68% below EMA200 and 14.28% below SMA50. Volume bias is down on daily timeframe. OBV daily trend is down. The wiki thesis confirms: 'short on bounces.' 3. ASYMMETRY MATH: Long setup: entry $44.36, nearest resistance $46.97 = $2.61 upside (+5.9%), nearest support $42.09 = $2.27 downside (-5.1%) = 1.15:1 — fails the 2:1 minimum bar. Short setup: entry $44.36, stop above resistance $46.97 = $2.61 risk, target $42.09 = $2.27 reward = 0.87:1 — even worse. Despite directional bearish lean (consistent with wiki), neither side clears the asymmetry threshold. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: RSI-7 at 15.36 signals extreme short-term oversold; a violent mean-reversion bounce (flagged in wiki as a tail scenario for shorts) could trigger a stop on a short. Daily bullish RSI divergence detected. MACD histogram turning positive. These create a noisy, treacherous environment for either direction. MACRO OVERRIDE NOTE: Even though macro is broadly risk-on, NKE is not participating — sector headwind from US-China trade tensions (Nike's supply chain exposure) offsets the macro tailwind. With no clean asymmetry in either direction and a name in confirmed structural decline, the correct call is neutral — wait for a better setup.

DIS.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with near-term caveats. S&P 500 at fresh all-time highs (7,200+), April 2026 best month since 2020. Iran peace talks driving WTI -2% — directly relevant for DIS as lower energy costs reduce consumer spending drag and improve entertainment spending appetite. Fed on hold at 3.5-3.75% with no near-term cuts, but regime is broadly supportive of consumer-facing names. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: DIS is outperforming on the 21-day window (+7.65% ROC-21d vs. S&P 500 finishing April strongly). Price is above EMA5, EMA10, EMA20, EMA21, EMA50, and all hourly MAs. Hourly EMAs are fully aligned uptrend. Daily MACD produced a bullish crossover. ADX at 37.34 with +DI (32.31) >> -DI (18.74) — strong directional trend. Apr 30 saw a big green candle (open $100.91, close $103.75) on 1.23x above-average volume, indicating institutional participation. Hourly OBV is rising and diverging from price (accumulation signal). 3. ASYMMETRY MATH: Entry at pullback to $102.50 (near hourly support cluster $101.97-$102.11 and hourly Fib 61.8% at $102.11), target $107.11 (tested resistance, 14 tests), stop $101.02 (volume-confirmed pivot low, 17 tests). Upside: $4.61 (+4.5%), Downside: $1.48 (-1.4%) = 3.1:1. Clears the 2:1 bar with room to spare. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION / RISK FLAGS: DIS is still below EMA200 ($105.49) and SMA200 ($109.33), meaning longer-term institutional trend is still down — this caps the bullish confidence. Daily OBV trend is down (distribution on daily, accumulation on hourly — a split). Q2 FY2026 earnings catalyst is in May 2026 per wiki, which is imminent; this creates binary event risk that can gap past stops. Earnings proximity limits the holding window and injects volatility risk. Confidence set at 58: the R/R math and short-term momentum are good, but below-EMA200 positioning, daily OBV distribution, and earnings-event risk cap the conviction. THESIS ALIGNMENT: Wiki notes 'finally reached streaming profitability' and a Q2 FY2026 earnings catalyst; this aligns with the bullish directional call — the catalyst is near.
